audit_log.check: Autonomy vs Automation: PASS (pattern recognition AI-driven, not fixed rules)
Consciousness Awareness: PASS (convection = consciousness elevation byproduct)
Quality Standards: PASS (genuine advocacy metrics, not manufactured virality)
No Manipulation: PASS (tracks organic sharing, doesn't exploit for forced virality)
Convection Economics: PASS (thrilled customers who share = core metric)

dependency.integration_points: Email content analysis for convection patterns
Story arcs typically generate highest convection

consciousness_profile.description: Convection (forwards, shares, testimonials) is natural byproduct of consciousness-matched content that deeply resonates. High convection = high resonance = consciousness elevation working.

convection_consciousness_correlation.principle: Content that elevates consciousness generates natural sharing (convection)

levels_1_3.share_format: Forward to colleague/friend with similar problem
levels_1_3.share_message: "Check this out - might help with X"

levels_4_6.share_trigger: Identity resonance ("This is exactly how I feel")
levels_4_6.share_format: Share in community, post about transformation
levels_4_6.share_message: "This really spoke to me - feeling seen"

levels_7_9.share_format: Cite in own content, reference in conversations
levels_7_9.share_message: "Brilliant framing that ties together X, Y, Z"

consciousness_profile.convection_notes: Convection economics = thrilled customers who share naturally; high convection = proof of value + consciousness elevation; convection compounds (viral loops)

purpose.primary: Track convection metrics (forwards, shares, testimonials, referrals) to measure viral heat
purpose.secondary: Identify patterns in high-convection emails (what creates sharing behavior)
purpose.tertiary: Visualize viral loops (email → share → new subscriber → repeat)

when_to_use.scenario: Measuring email performance beyond opens/clicks (convection = true engagement)
Identifying which emails generate most sharing/advocacy
Optimizing content for viral potential (learn from high-convection emails)
Tracking referral attribution (which emails drive new subscribers)

not_for.item: Tracking individual sharing behavior invasively (privacy violation)
Replacing quality metrics (convection supplements, not replaces opens/clicks)

principle.statement: People share what genuinely helps/resonates. High convection = proof content delivers real value, not just gets opens.
principle.rationale: Forwards/shares = social capital investment; people only share what makes THEM look good; convection = higher bar than opens

principle.statement: Track natural sharing behavior, don't manipulate for viral tricks. Convection emerges from value, not gimmicks.
principle.rationale: Constitutional compliance (consent_intent); forced virality = short-term spike, long-term damage; organic sharing = sustainable growth

principle.statement: Shared emails create new subscribers → who share → who create more subscribers. Compounding effect over time.
principle.rationale: Viral coefficient >1.0 = exponential growth; track loops to understand compound effect; convection = leverage at scale

principle.statement: Analyze high-convection emails to identify patterns (topics, formats, hooks). Replicate winning elements.
principle.rationale: Convection isn't random; specific patterns generate sharing; extract patterns, apply to new content, repeat

principle.statement: Convection economics: Thrilled customers who share naturally are better than ads, funnels, paid acquisition.
principle.rationale: Customer acquisition cost (CAC) = $0 from referrals; lifetime value (LTV) higher from referred customers; sustainable business model

metric.definition: Total social shares (Twitter, LinkedIn, Facebook, etc.)
metric.tracking: Use UTM parameters or social share tracking tools

metric.definition: Unsolicited replies mentioning "shared this", "forwarded to friend", etc.
metric.tracking: Manual review OR keyword detection in replies

metric.attribution: Track referral source in signup (if available)

metric.definition: Positive/negative tone of replies (manual or AI analysis)
metric.high_convection_signal: Effusive replies ("This changed my thinking!") indicate sharing likelihood

standard.formula: K = (Subscribers who share) × (New subscribers per share)

interpretation.k_less_than_1: K < 1.0: Declining growth (each subscriber brings <1 new subscriber)
interpretation.k_equals_1: K = 1.0: Linear growth (each subscriber brings 1 new subscriber)

example.scenario: 1,000 opens, 20 forwards (2%), each forward brings 0.5 new subscribers

standard.definition: Composite score combining all convection metrics

guardrail.rule: Track ONLY organic sharing; do NOT manipulate with forced virality tactics
guardrail.prohibited: Incentivized sharing ("Forward to 3 friends to get X"), spam tactics, fake viral loops
guardrail.allowed: Trackable share buttons, referral attribution, natural forwarding behavior

guardrail.rule: Track aggregate metrics, not individual sharing behavior invasively
guardrail.allowed: Total forwards, share counts, referral sources
guardrail.prohibited: Tracking WHO individual shares to, invasive monitoring of personal networks

guardrail.rule: Testimonials must be genuine, unsolicited feedback (not manufactured)
guardrail.genuine: Subscriber replies saying "I shared this with my team"
guardrail.manufactured: Asking "Will you share this?" in every email (forced behavior)

framework.purpose: Standardized metrics for measuring viral heat

forward_rate.data_source: Email platform analytics (if available) OR UTM tracking
forward_rate.improvement_targets: Increase forward rate by identifying/replicating high-convection patterns

share_count.definition: Total shares on social media (Twitter, LinkedIn, Facebook, etc.)

tracking_methods.method: Social share buttons with UTM tracking
Social listening tools (mentions of email content)
Manual monitoring (search for email quotes on social)

share_count.note: Harder to track than forwards, but indicates public advocacy (higher bar)

testimonial_mentions.manual_review: Best tracked via manual review of replies (AI keyword detection as supplement)

referral_rate.calculation: (New subscribers from referrals / Total new subscribers) × 100

attribution.method: Signup question: "How did you hear about us?"

referral_rate.high_convection_signal: If >20% new subscribers from referrals = high convection system

high_convection_signals.signal: Sharing intent ("Sending to my whole team")

framework.purpose: Identify patterns in high-convection emails to replicate success

pattern.description: Content that challenges conventional wisdom, reveals unexpected truth

examples.ex: "The mistake that actually increased my sales"

pattern.why_shareable: Challenges assumptions, makes sharer look insightful, conversation-starter

pattern.description: Concise, memorable phrases that stand alone (tweetable)

examples.ex: "Convection economics: Thrilled customers who share > paid ads"
"People don't buy information. They buy transformation."

pattern.why_shareable: Easy to extract, attribute, share; makes sharer look smart/aligned
pattern.formatting_tip: Use line breaks, italics, or quotes to make quotable lines stand out visually

pattern.description: Specific numbers that challenge expectations or reveal patterns

examples.ex: "Story-based emails get 2-3x more forwards than promotional emails"
"92% of readers skip bullet points longer than 2 sentences"

pattern.why_shareable: Data = credibility; surprises = conversation; specific numbers = memorable
pattern.sourcing_note: Source must be credible (own research, cited study, clear methodology)

pattern.description: Content that reframes entire category or approach

examples.ex: "Email isn't about opens. It's about consciousness elevation."
"Stop asking 'How do I get more subscribers?' Ask 'How do I create thrilled advocates?'"

pattern.why_shareable: Provides new language, mental model; positions sharer as thought leader

examples.ex: "The 5-minute subject line formula that doubled my opens"
"Change this one word in your CTA (3x more clicks)"

pattern.why_shareable: Practical value, fast ROI, easy to implement; sharer provides help

pattern.description: Authentic personal narrative with struggle → insight → transformation

examples.ex: "The 2am panic attack that changed my business"
"Losing 10k subscribers taught me this about value"

pattern.why_shareable: Vulnerability = connection; relatability = "me too" effect; lessons = value

pattern_extraction_process.step: 1. Identify high-convection emails (forward rate >1%, testimonial mentions, shares)
2. Analyze content for patterns (counterintuitive, quotable, data, paradigm, quick win, story)
3. Extract specific elements (exact phrases, formats, structures)
4. Replicate in new content (apply patterns to different topics)
5. Test and measure (did replication generate similar convection?)
6. Iterate (refine patterns, add new patterns, repeat)

framework.purpose: Map how shared emails create new subscribers who share, creating exponential growth

stage.attribution: Track via referral links or signup source

stage.metric: New subscribers → future sends → opens → forwards → more new subscribers
stage.compounding: Each email builds on previous; 10 emails = cumulative viral growth

framework.visualization_example: Email 1: 1,000 sends → 350 opens → 7 forwards → 4 new subscribers Email 2: 1,004 sends → 351 opens → 7 forwards → 4 new subscribers Email 3: 1,008 sends → 353 opens → 7 forwards → 4 new subscribers ... Email 52 (1 year): 1,208 sends (20.8% growth from viral loop alone)

lever.strategy: Apply convection patterns (counterintuitive, quotable, data, etc.)
lever.impact: 2% → 3% forward rate = 50% more new subscribers per email

lever.strategy: Optimize referral landing page, signup flow
lever.impact: 50% → 75% conversion = 50% more new subscribers per forward

lever.strategy: Send more high-convection emails (without burning list)
lever.impact: Weekly → 2x/week = 2x viral loop cycles per month

framework.purpose: Real-time view of email convection performance

trend_analysis.metric: Convection trend over time (improving or declining?)
trend_analysis.alert: If forward rate drops >25% month-over-month → investigate content quality

pattern_frequency.metric: Which convection patterns appear most in high-performing emails?
pattern_frequency.insight: If "counterintuitive insights" appears in 8/10 top emails → double down on that pattern

visualization_types.viz: Line chart: Forward rate over time
Bar chart: Heat index per email (last 20 emails)
Funnel: Viral loop stages (sends → opens → forwards → new subs)
Pie chart: Convection pattern distribution in top emails

orchestration_steps.step: 1. Collect convection data per email (forwards, shares, testimonials, referrals, sentiment)
2. Calculate convection metrics using calculate_convection_score() tool
3. Identify high-convection emails (Heat Index >7, forward rate >1.5%)
5. Extract winning elements (topics, formats, phrases, structures)
7. Track viral loop using track_viral_indicators() tool
8. Monitor dashboard for trends, optimize based on insights
9. Iterate: Test new patterns, measure, refine, repeat

heuristics.rule: IF forward_rate >2% THEN analyze email for patterns + replicate in future content
IF Heat_Index >8 THEN mark as "Convection Winner" + extract all elements for pattern library
IF viral_coefficient >0.05 THEN viral loop is strong + increase email frequency (more cycles)
IF convection declining (>25% drop) THEN audit content for pattern absence + reintroduce winning patterns
IF testimonial mentions increase THEN content resonating deeply + consciousness elevation likely occurring

tool.purpose: Calculate Heat Index (0-10) from convection metrics

inputs.input: email_metrics (dict with forward_rate, share_count, testimonial_count, referral_rate, sentiment_score)

tool.validation_logic:
def calculate_convection_score(email_metrics): """ Calculates Heat Index from convection metrics.
Weights: - Forward rate: 30% - Share count: 20% - Testimonial mentions: 20% - Referral rate: 20% - Reply sentiment: 10%
Returns: dict: { 'heat_index': float (0-10), 'viral_coefficient': float, 'convection_level': str, 'breakdown': dict, 'recommendations': list } """ forward_rate = email_metrics.get('forward_rate', 0.0) # Percentage share_count = email_metrics.get('share_count', 0) testimonial_count = email_metrics.get('testimonial_count', 0) referral_rate = email_metrics.get('referral_rate', 0.0) # Percentage sentiment_score = email_metrics.get('sentiment_score', 5.0) # 0-10 scale
opens = email_metrics.get('opens', 1000) # Default for calculation
# Normalize each metric to 0-10 scale # Forward rate: 0% = 0, 5% = 10 forward_score = min((forward_rate / 5.0) * 10, 10)
# Share count: Normalized per 100 opens (0 shares = 0, 5+ shares = 10) shares_per_100 = (share_count / opens) * 100 if opens > 0 else 0 share_score = min((shares_per_100 / 5.0) * 10, 10)
# Testimonial count: Normalized per 100 opens (0 = 0, 3+ = 10) testimonials_per_100 = (testimonial_count / opens) * 100 if opens > 0 else 0 testimonial_score = min((testimonials_per_100 / 3.0) * 10, 10)
# Referral rate: 0% = 0, 20% = 10 referral_score = min((referral_rate / 20.0) * 10, 10)
# Sentiment already 0-10 scale
# Weighted average heat_index = ( forward_score * 0.30 + share_score * 0.20 + testimonial_score * 0.20 + referral_score * 0.20 + sentiment_score * 0.10 )
# Viral coefficient (simplified) # K = (forwards / opens) × (new subs per forward) # Assume ~0.5 new subs per forward if referral_rate provided if referral_rate > 0: new_subs_per_forward = 0.5 # Conservative estimate viral_coefficient = (forward_rate / 100) * new_subs_per_forward else: viral_coefficient = 0.0
# Convection level if heat_index < 4: convection_level = 'cold' elif heat_index < 7: convection_level = 'warm' elif heat_index < 9: convection_level = 'hot' else: convection_level = 'supernova'
breakdown = { 'forward_score': round(forward_score, 2), 'share_score': round(share_score, 2), 'testimonial_score': round(testimonial_score, 2), 'referral_score': round(referral_score, 2), 'sentiment_score': round(sentiment_score, 2) }
recommendations = [] if forward_score < 3: recommendations.append('Low forward rate - apply convection patterns (counterintuitive, quotable, data)') if share_score < 3: recommendations.append('Low social shares - add quotable lines, make content screenshot-worthy') if convection_level == 'hot' or convection_level == 'supernova': recommendations.append('HIGH CONVECTION - Analyze this email for patterns to replicate!')
return { 'heat_index': round(heat_index, 1), 'viral_coefficient': round(viral_coefficient, 4), 'convection_level': convection_level, 'breakdown': breakdown, 'recommendations': recommendations }

tool.purpose: Track viral loop stages and calculate viral coefficient
Analyze email content for convection patterns (counterintuitive, quotable, data, etc.)
tool.validation_logic:
import re
def identify_convection_patterns(email_content): """ Identifies convection patterns in email content.
Patterns: 1. Counterintuitive insights 2. Quotable lines 3. Surprising statistics 4. Paradigm shifts 5. Quick wins 6. Personal stories
Returns: dict: { 'patterns_detected': list, 'pattern_count': int, 'quotable_lines': list, 'convection_potential': str } """ patterns = [] quotable_lines = []
text_lower = email_content.lower()
# Pattern 1: Counterintuitive counterintuitive_phrases = [ r'the mistake that (?:actually|surprisingly)', r'why .* (?:don\'t|doesn\'t) work', r'the (?:flaw|bug) that (?:actually|becomes)', r'counter-?intuitively' ] if any(re.search(p, text_lower) for p in counterintuitive_phrases): patterns.append('counterintuitive_insight')
# Pattern 2: Quotable lines (short, punchy sentences with strong claims) sentences = re.split(r'[.!?]\s+', email_content) for sent in sentences: # Quotable if: 40-150 chars, contains strong word, complete thought if 40 <= len(sent) <= 150: strong_words = ['better', 'best', 'never', 'always', 'only', 'true', 'real'] if any(word in sent.lower() for word in strong_words): quotable_lines.append(sent.strip())
if quotable_lines: patterns.append('quotable_lines')
# Pattern 3: Surprising statistics (specific numbers + percentage/multiplier) stats_patterns = [ r'\d+(?:\.\d+)?%', # Percentages r'\d+x', # Multipliers (2x, 10x) r'\d+/\d+', # Ratios (1/50) r'\d+ out of \d+' # Ratios (1 out of 50) ] if any(re.search(p, email_content) for p in stats_patterns): patterns.append('surprising_statistics')
# Pattern 4: Paradigm shift (reframing language) paradigm_phrases = [ r'(?:stop|don\'t) (?:asking|thinking|doing)', r'(?:it\'s not|isn\'t) about .* it\'s about', r'instead of .* try', r'new (?:mental model|framework|approach)' ] if any(re.search(p, text_lower) for p in paradigm_phrases): patterns.append('paradigm_shift')
# Pattern 5: Quick win (time-bound, actionable) quick_win_phrases = [ r'\d+ (?:minute|hour|day)', r'quick (?:win|fix|hack)', r'immediately', r'right now', r'today' ] if any(re.search(p, text_lower) for p in quick_win_phrases): patterns.append('quick_win')
# Pattern 6: Personal story (first-person narrative) personal_story_indicators = [ r'\bi\b.*(?:learned|discovered|realized|struggled)', r'my (?:biggest|worst|first)', r'when i (?:lost|failed|hit bottom)' ] if any(re.search(p, text_lower) for p in personal_story_indicators): patterns.append('personal_story')
pattern_count = len(patterns)
# Convection potential based on pattern count if pattern_count == 0: convection_potential = 'low' elif pattern_count <= 2: convection_potential = 'medium' else: convection_potential = 'high'
return { 'patterns_detected': patterns, 'pattern_count': pattern_count, 'quotable_lines': quotable_lines[:5], # Top 5 'convection_potential': convection_potential }

tool.purpose: Predict convection potential BEFORE sending email
tool.validation_logic:
def predict_viral_potential(email_content, subject_line): """ Predicts convection potential before sending.
Factors: - Convection patterns present - Subject line shareability - Content quotability - Actionability
Returns: dict: { 'predicted_heat_index': float (0-10), 'viral_signals': list, 'optimization_suggestions': list } """ score = 5.0 # Start neutral viral_signals = [] suggestions = []
# Get patterns from content patterns_result = identify_convection_patterns(email_content) pattern_count = patterns_result['pattern_count']
# Pattern count factor if pattern_count >= 3: score += 2.0 viral_signals.append('High pattern count (3+) - strong convection potential') elif pattern_count >= 1: score += 1.0 viral_signals.append('Moderate pattern count (1-2)') else: score -= 1.0 suggestions.append('Add convection patterns (counterintuitive, quotable, data)')
# Quotable lines if len(patterns_result['quotable_lines']) >= 3: score += 1.5 viral_signals.append('Multiple quotable lines (screenshot-worthy)') elif len(patterns_result['quotable_lines']) >= 1: score += 0.5 else: suggestions.append('Add quotable one-liners (40-150 chars, punchy)')
# Subject line shareability (curiosity + specificity) subject_lower = subject_line.lower() if len(subject_line) >= 30 and len(subject_line) <= 60: score += 0.5
if any(word in subject_lower for word in ['surprising', 'counterintuitive', 'weird', 'unexpected']): score += 1.0 viral_signals.append('Subject has curiosity element')
# Content length (sweet spot 400-800 words) word_count = len(email_content.split()) if 400 <= word_count <= 800: score += 0.5 elif word_count < 300: score -= 0.5 suggestions.append('Email too short - add depth (400-800 words ideal)') elif word_count > 1200: score -= 0.5 suggestions.append('Email too long - trim to 400-800 words for shareability')
# Actionability (CTA, implementation instructions) if 'here\'s how' in email_content.lower() or 'try this' in email_content.lower(): score += 0.5 viral_signals.append('Actionable content (implementation guidance)')
predicted_heat_index = max(0, min(score, 10.0))
# Overall recommendation if predicted_heat_index >= 7: viral_signals.append('HIGH viral potential - expect strong convection') elif predicted_heat_index < 5: suggestions.append('Overall: Low viral potential - consider adding 2-3 convection patterns')
return { 'predicted_heat_index': round(predicted_heat_index, 1), 'viral_signals': viral_signals, 'optimization_suggestions': suggestions }

inputs.input: email_history (list of dicts with convection metrics per email)
